history.titleZh: 英特尔向爱尔兰晶圆厂投资57亿美元，旨在提升基于Intel 3工艺的Xeon 6及下一代Xeon产品产量
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔本周宣布，将向位于爱尔兰莱克斯利普（Leixlip）附近的制造工厂投资50亿欧元（约合57亿美元），用于扩建和现代化其生产运营。该项目旨在提升英特尔Xeon 6处理器以及基于英特尔3制造工艺（3纳米级）的下一代英特尔Xeon产品的产能，同时支持该基地的先进研发（R&D）活动。

此次升级主要针对Fab 34工厂，将安装新设备，并进行广泛的设施基础设施改进，以提高制造效率。项目的关键要素之一是扩建厂区的自动化物料运输网络，将独立的制造模块连接成一个高速生产系统。值得注意的是，此次现代化改造不涉及洁净室扩建。英特尔预计，升级将使莱克斯利普基地能够生产更大规模的基于英特尔3工艺的产品，并更好地利用现有洁净室容量。

英特尔于2023年在莱克斯利普附近启用了Fab 34工厂，并一直在该基地使用依赖极紫外光刻（EUV）的工艺技术，生产包括采用英特尔4工艺的Core Ultra 100系列以及采用英特尔3工艺的Xeon 6在内的多种芯片。目前，英特尔Fab 34是欧洲唯一一家使用EUV工具进行高产量半导体制造的设施。

2024年中，英特尔因急需资金，宣布以101亿欧元的价格将Fab 34的49%股权出售给Apollo Global Management。今年4月，该公司宣布将以142亿美元回购Fab 34的49%股权，为当前的扩建和投资铺平了道路。英特尔声称该项目已于今年早些时候启动，但未透露升级完成的具体时间。

英特尔执行副总裁、首席技术与运营官兼英特尔代工总经理Naga Chandrasekaran表示：“通过用最先进的技术投资我们现有的晶圆厂，并安装尖端工具，我们不仅增加了关键产品（如基于英特尔3工艺的Xeon 6和下一代英特尔Xeon处理器）的产量，还确保爱尔兰保持在世界上最先进的制造生态系统的前沿，同时加强该地区在全球科技格局中的角色。”

此外，英特尔表示，这项投资将通过增加国内尖端CPU的产量，强化欧洲半导体供应链，并支持欧盟的技术主权目标。不过，这一声明存在一个关键细节：所有在爱尔兰生产的硅片都会被运回美国进行测试和封装，最终产品如Core Ultra或Xeon 6实际上被标记为“美国制造”。

history.title: New York enacts one-year data center ban on projects larger than 50 megawatts — first US state to implement moratorium; will also pursue repealing tax exemptions
history.description: New York is the first to pass a statewide data center moratorium, which pauses all projects greater than 50 MW for one year. The governor's office said that it will create a GEIS to hold developments to "consistent standards," and also study the environmental impact of the construction and operation of data centers.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter New York Governor Kathy Hochul signed into law Senate Bill S10642 today, also called the Responsible Data Center Development Act, which would put a one-year moratorium on all data center developments in the state. According to Reuters, this is the first temporary ban to be enforced statewide in the U.S. Maine’s legislature was actually the first one to pass a statewide moratorium, but Maine Governor Janet Mills vetoed the measure after it failed to exempt a data center project “that enjoys strong local support from its host community and region.”Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrow"As data center development threatens ⁠to hike up utility bills, deplete our natural resources, and create uncertainty for New Yorkers, it's my responsibility to take ​action and lead," Hochul said in a statement. She also said that she’s pursuing legislation to repeal tax exemptions for large data centers. The moratorium will apply to data center projects with a capacity of 50 megawatts or more, with the New York Department of Environmental Conservation not issuing any more permits to projects that haven’t been completed yet.Hochul said that the state will build a Generic Environmental Impact Statement (GEIS) so that future data center developments are held to “consistent standards,” while also looking at how the construction and operation of these projects will impact the environment. Although the data center moratorium is set for one year, the governor’s office said that it will be lifted once the state has finalized the GEIS.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: President Donald Trump has been pushing for the development of AI technologies, with the White House releasing the ‘AI Action Plan’ to accelerate infrastructure build-out. While this policy encouraged the development of AI data centers, the numerous large projects also resulted in memory and storage chip shortages, as well as negative impacts in the communities and regions where they’re located. For example, Monitoring Analytics, which oversees the largest power grid operator in the U.S., attributes an “irreversible” 76% price hike to increased data center demand, while a Virginia county has asked government offices to conserve power because of AI-driven price hikes. There have also been multiple issues with various data center projects relating to water consumption and air and noise pollution, which is why 70% of Americans now oppose having a data center built near their home.All this pushback has resulted in many jurisdictions passing data center moratoriums. More than 75 projects have already been delayed in the first half of this year, amounting to $130 billion, with New York state being the first one to pass a state-wide temporary ban. Before this, Seattle, which plays host to the headquarters of Amazon and Microsoft, passed a similar one-year moratorium last month. You may like Texas county passes data center ban for rural areas for a year, move comes in wake of AI data centers moving to remote areas to skirt regulations Seattle to pass one-year AI data center moratorium next week Multiple small Tennessee counties pass temporary data center bans While delays like this will likely negatively impact the future availability of compute within the U.S., it’s also forcing tech giants to speed up innovations and governments to pass laws that protect their constituents. Multiple startups have started unveiling or even turning on their small modular reactor (SMR) prototypes, which could deliver the power needed by data centers without increasing air pollution or straining the local power grid. AI tech companies like Microsoft and Nvidia are also working on solutions that will cut data center energy use and water consumption, while Oregon’s POWER Act increased data center bills by 30% while cutting residential costs by 1.3%. history.titleZh: 纽约对超过50兆瓦的数据中心项目实施为期一年的禁令——成为美国首个实施暂停令的州；还将寻求废除税收豁免
history.descriptionZh:
纽约州州长凯西·霍楚尔近日签署了《负责任数据中心发展法案》（参议院法案S10642），在全州范围内实施为期一年的数据中心建设禁令。这是美国首个由州政府强制执行的此类临时禁令。此前缅因州立法机构曾率先通过全州暂停令，但因未豁免一个“受到当地社区和地区强烈支持”的数据中心项目，遭州长否决。该法案规定，新建或扩建容量达50兆瓦及以上的数据中心项目将暂停审批，纽约州环境保护局将不再为尚未完工的项目发放许可证。霍楚尔表示，此举旨在应对数据中心开发可能导致的电费上涨、自然资源枯竭以及对居民生活的不确定性影响。她同时推动立法取消大型数据中心的税收减免政策。纽约州将编制一份《通用环境影响声明》，确保未来数据中心开发符合统一标准，并评估施工和运营对环境的影响。该禁令为期一年，一旦GEIS最终确定将立即解除。

背景方面，白宫发布的“AI行动计划”加速了人工智能基础设施建设，但大规模数据中心项目引发了一系列连锁反应：存储和内存芯片短缺、社区环境恶化。例如，美国最大电网运营商（PJM Interconnection）的监管机构Monitoring Analytics指出，数据中心需求增长导致了“不可逆转的”76%电价上涨；弗吉尼亚州一个县因AI驱动的电价飙升要求政府办公室节约用电。此外，数据中心项目还面临水资源消耗、空气和噪音污染等问题，70%的美国民众反对在住宅附近建设数据中心。这些阻力已导致今年上半年超过75个项目被推迟，涉及1300亿美元投资。

技术原理与创新方面，虽然原文未深入涉及芯片设计，但指出科技巨头正加速创新以应对能源瓶颈：多家初创公司已推出或启动小型模块化反应堆原型，可为数据中心提供不增加空气污染、不加重电网负担的电力；微软和英伟达等AI公司正研发降低数据中心能耗和水耗的技术方案。俄勒冈州的《POWER法案》则将数据中心电费提高30%，同时使居民电费降低1.3%。这些举措试图在算力需求与环境保护之间寻求平衡。

性能参数与对比数据：纽约州禁令门槛为50兆瓦，涵盖大型数据中心；Seattle（亚马逊和微软总部所在地）上月也通过了类似的一年期暂停令；德州一县针对农村地区发布了数据中心禁令。这些地方性法规虽短期可能影响美国国内算力可用性，但也倒逼技术迭代和政策完善。

技术影响与应用场景：该法案的直接影响是暂停新建大型AI数据中心，迫使企业重新评估选址策略，加速绿色能源和高效冷却技术（如液冷、光互联）的研发。同时，小型模块化反应堆等清洁电力方案有望成为数据中心供电的替代选择。长期看，统一的GEIS标准将为未来数据中心的环境合规提供明确框架，保护地方社区利益，避免“先建后批”的混乱局面。此外，纽约州的举措可能引发其他州效仿，推动整个产业向更可持续的方向演进。

history.title: Nvidia slashes list of authorized customers in Asia in a bid to reduce AI chip smuggling, report claims — company sent field inspectors, called customers to check if business is genuine after pressure from Washington
history.description: The company culled its list of verified customers, cutting out more than half of its existing client list to reduce incidents of smuggling. Remaining clients have passed more stringent checks, including physical inspections of data centers and interviews with end users.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AI tech giant Nvidia, which builds some of the most coveted AI chips in the world, has reportedly created a new “whitelist” of verified companies to help prevent its products from getting smuggled into China. According to the Financial Times, this roster cuts the number of authorized clients by more than half, with those remaining having passed tougher compliance inspections to ensure that they are genuine businesses, not shell companies designed to forward Nvidia GPUs and servers into China. Some of the steps that Nvidia took to help safeguard its chips reportedly included sending staff to customer data centers, contract verification, and interviewing end users.Go deeper with TH Premium: Taiwan, trade, and tariffs (Image credit: tsmc)China's latest round of rare-earth export controls explainedAnalyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rulesU.S. government plans tariff exemptions for TSMCNvidia wants China's market share to secure the future of CUDA in the regionSources told the publication that the company made this move after Washington pressured it into tightening its legal compliance, which comes months after the arrest of Supermicro co-founder Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw, alongside two other suspects, for allegedly smuggling $2.5 billion worth of Nvidia hardware into China. This clampdown also extended into Singapore, which saw the seizure of a $42-million mansion tied to alleged AI GPU smugglers, and Taiwan, where authorities raided the offices of Supermicro and two supply-chain partners as part of a chip smuggling probe. Nvidia was not immediately available for comment on the news.Although the U.S. has banned the latest AI GPUs for export into China since 2022, various investigations showed Chinese companies could still easily get their hands on these coveted chips until recently. Washington’s and its allies’ crackdown on AI GPU smuggling have cut supply in China, which is now making it harder for AI companies to procure the processors they need. President Donald Trump took a 180-degree turn in December 2025 and finally allowed Nvidia to export its H200 GPUs to select customers in the region, which would have alleviated the situation. However, Beijing refused to allow Chinese companies to buy these AI processors — instead, it’s banking on domestic semiconductor manufacturers to make up for the shortfall, but it’s apparently still not enough. One tech executive even told the Financial Times that all domestic suppliers are sold out and that they’re even considering less powerful chips, as long as they could be put to use.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: As Nvidia reportedly cleaned up its verified list of clients and made it harder for non-vetted companies to acquire its chips, the company has also told its partners to fix their export control compliance. “We insist our partners are compliant,” Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang told the media last May after Taiwan started its operations against AI chip smuggling into China. “We hope that they will enhance and improve their regulation compliance and prevent that from happening in the future.” Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds. history.titleZh: 据报道，英伟达大幅削减亚洲授权客户名单以减少AI芯片走私——在华盛顿施压
history.descriptionZh:
英伟达（Nvidia）近期创建了一份新的“白名单”机制，以应对美国政府日益严格的出口管制压力，防止其高端AI芯片通过空壳公司等渠道走私进入中国。据《金融时报》报道，这份名单将授权客户数量削减了超过一半，只有通过更严格合规审查的实体才能保留资格。审查措施包括派遣员工前往客户的数据中心进行实地检查、验证合同文件，以及直接访谈最终用户，以确保这些客户是真实的商业企业，而非专门用于向中国转运英伟达GPU和服务器设备的壳公司。

这一合规严控行动的背景，源于美国政府对高端AI芯片出口中国的持续禁令（自2022年起实施），以及近期一系列打击走私的行动。数月前，超微电脑（Supermicro）联合创始人廖益贤（Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw）因涉嫌将价值25亿美元的英伟达硬件走私至中国而被捕，另有两人同时被拘押。此后，执法行动扩展至新加坡，当局扣押了一栋价值4200万美元的豪宅，该豪宅被指与AI GPU走私集团有关；台湾地区也对超微电脑及其两家供应链合作伙伴的办公室进行了突击搜查，作为芯片走私调查的一部分。华盛顿及其盟友的持续打击，已显著削减了流入中国的AI芯片供应，使得中国AI公司更难获取所需的处理器。

尽管美国实施了严格的出口限制，但多项调查表明，直到最近中国公司仍能相对轻易地获取这些受管控的芯片。英伟达此次收紧客户名单，正是对华盛顿施加压力的直接回应。值得注意的是，2025年12月，时任总统特朗普曾做出政策转向，允许英伟达向中国特定客户出口H200 GPU，这原本可能缓解供应紧张。然而，北京方面拒绝了中国企业购买这些处理器的许可申请，转而大力扶持国内半导体制造商以填补缺口。但一位科技行业高管向《金融时报》表示，国内所有供应商的产能均已售罄，企业甚至开始考虑采购性能较低的芯片，只要能够投入使用即可。

英伟达在清理验证客户名单、提高非审查企业获取芯片难度的同时，也已告知其合作伙伴必须修复出口管制合规漏洞。“我们坚持要求合作伙伴遵守规定，”英伟达CEO黄仁勋在去年5月台湾地区启动打击AI芯片走私行动后对媒体表示，“我们希望他们能够加强和改进监管合规，防止此类事件再次发生。”

从技术影响和应用场景来看，这一白名单机制本质上是一种供应链合规管理创新，它通过多层实地验证和合同审计，从源头上切断芯片流向灰色市场的途径。虽然不涉及芯片架构本身的革新，但它直接影响了全球AI算力分配格局：中国AI公司（如大模型训练、自动驾驶、云计算等领域）获取高端英伟达GPU的难度进一步加大，迫使其转向国产替代方案或更低性能的芯片。与此同时，英伟达在维护其全球最大AI芯片市场占有率与遵守美国出口管制之间，正面临日益严峻的合规博弈。这一事件也凸显了美国出口管制政策在执行层面的持续收紧，以及全球AI算力供应链因地缘政治而出现的深刻分裂。

history.title: Researchers create programmable material that can steer heat and remember its state without power — breakthrough could eventually aid AI chip cooling and silicon photonics
history.description: Researchers created a programmable thermal material that steers heat and retains its state without power, a breakthrough that could benefit AI chips, silicon photonics, and infrared devices.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Researchers from Osaka Metropolitan University have developed a programmable thermal device that can control where heat is radiated while remembering its configuration even after power is removed, a capability that could one day contribute to smarter thermal management in high-performance chips, silicon photonics, infrared sensors, and energy-harvesting systems. The work, published in Laser & Photonics Reviews, overcomes two longstanding obstacles that have prevented the practical realization of nonreciprocal thermal devices.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingThe device combines a magneto-optical material — a material that changes its optical properties in the presence of a magnetic field — with a phase-change material known as germanium-antimony-tellurium (GST) to independently control how a surface absorbs and emits infrared radiation. Unlike previous designs that lost their functionality once power was removed or only worked when light struck the surface at extreme angles, the researchers say their device operates almost straight on while retaining its programmed state without continuous energy input.Under normal circumstances, materials follow a principle stating that if a surface efficiently absorbs heat at a particular wavelength and direction, it must also emit heat equally well under the same conditions. This relationship, defined by Kirchhoff's law of thermal radiation, holds for conventional materials and limits how precisely engineers can manipulate heat. Rather than directing thermal energy where it is most useful, these materials simply emit heat based on how they absorb it.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Circumventing this relationship has become an active area of research, as it could give engineers an entirely new way to control thermal energy. Devices capable of independently steering absorption and emission could improve radiative cooling, thermophotovoltaic systems that convert heat into electricity, infrared sensing, thermal communication, and other photonic technologies where controlling heat is just as important as controlling light.Researchers have explored several ways to achieve this by breaking Lorentz reciprocity, the physical principle that links incoming and outgoing electromagnetic waves. Most approaches rely on magneto-optical materials, magnetic Weyl semimetals, or actively modulated metasurfaces. However, these designs have generally encountered two major problems. First, they require light to strike the surface at very oblique, or grazing, angles to produce strong directional behavior. While this works experimentally, it significantly reduces the amount of usable thermal radiation and produces broad, inefficient emission patterns. Second, many existing designs are volatile. Their behavior disappears as soon as the magnetic field, electrical signal, or heating source controlling them is removed, making continuous power necessary simply to maintain their operating state. You may like Laser-driven spintronic memory device switches 1,000 times faster than DRAM Researchers build brain-like memory device for AI sensors that may improve energy efficiency Scientists create electronic devices that function reliably at extreme temps ranging from 500 degrees Celcuis to absolute zero The Osaka Metropolitan University team tackled both limitations by combining two materials that perform complementary roles. The first is indium arsenide (InAs), a magneto-optical semiconductor whose interaction with infrared light changes in the presence of a magnetic field. Rather than allowing light to behave identically in all directions, the material introduces a directional asymmetry that enables nonreciprocal thermal behavior. The second ingredient is GST, a phase-change material that can reversibly switch between amorphous and crystalline states, dramatically changing its optical properties while retaining whichever state it is written into, even after power is removed.The researchers patterned GST into a microscopic grating above the InAs layer, forming what they describe as a magneto-optical metagrating. The InAs provides the directional control needed to separate heat absorption from heat emission, while the GST layer acts as a non-volatile switch that stores the device's operating mode. Applying a magnetic field tunes how infrared radiation interacts with the structure, while changing the phase of the GST permanently alters that behavior until it is intentionally rewritten. In effect, the device can be programmed to emit heat differently and retain that configuration without requiring continuous energy.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsAccording to the researchers, the prototype achieved a nonreciprocity factor approaching 0.9 while operating at an incidence angle of just three degrees, much closer to normal incidence than the steep angles typically required by previous designs. The system also supports continuous tuning via changes in the magnetic field or incident angle, as well as digital on-off switching via the GST phase transition. The team further analyzed why the nonreciprocal effect weakens when GST changes state, concluding that the reduction results from a combination of optical field redistribution and increased damping rather than simple absorption losses alone.Although the technology remains an early-stage research demonstration, the ability to program thermal radiation could eventually become valuable in computing hardware as processors continue to pack more transistors, chiplets, and photonic components into increasingly compact packages. Future thermal metasurfaces could give engineers another tool for directing heat away from hotspots, reducing thermal interference between neighboring chiplets, or stabilizing silicon photonic devices whose optical characteristics shift with temperature.Beyond computing, the researchers also envision applications in radiative cooling, thermophotovoltaic energy conversion, infrared emitters, thermal communication systems, and photonic memory technologies. For now, however, the work remains a laboratory demonstration rather than a deployable technology. Considerable engineering challenges remain before programmable thermal emitters find their way into commercial electronics. history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: 研究人员创造出可编程材料，无需电力即可引导热量并记忆状态——这一突破有望最终助力AI芯片散热与硅光子学
history.descriptionZh:
大阪公立大学的研究团队成功开发出一种可编程热辐射器件，能够精确控制红外辐射的方向，并在断电后依然保持其配置状态。这项突破性成果发表在《激光与光子学评论》上，解决了长期阻碍非互易热辐射器件实际应用的两个关键难题：一是传统设计需要光线以极端掠射角入射才能工作，二是多数设计在电源移除后功能即消失。新器件能在近乎垂直入射（仅3度角）的情况下工作，且无需持续供电即可保持编程状态，有望为高性能芯片、硅光子学、红外传感器和能量收集系统带来更智能的热管理方案。

## 核心技术原理与创新

该器件的核心创新在于将两种互补材料巧妙结合：

1. **磁光材料——砷化铟（InAs）**：这是一种半导体，其光学特性会随外加磁场变化。在磁场作用下，InAs引入方向性不对称，使红外辐射的吸收和发射行为不再对称，从而打破传统的洛伦兹互易性——即通常材料吸收和发射热辐射必须相等的物理定律（基尔霍夫热辐射定律）。

2. **相变材料——锗锑碲（GST）**：这是一种可在非晶态和晶态之间可逆切换的材料，切换后其光学性质发生巨大变化，且不论切换至何种状态，断电后都能永久保持。GST充当非易失性开关，存储器件的操作模式。

研究团队将GST加工成微米级光栅，覆盖在InAs层上方，形成所谓的“磁光超光栅”（magneto-optical metagrating）。InAs提供方向控制，使热吸收与发射分离；GST层则作为非易失性开关，永久保留器件的热辐射行为，直到被重新编程写入。简单来说，该器件可以“编程”为以不同方式发射热量，并且无需持续供电就能记住这个设置。

## 性能参数与对比数据

实验原型的关键性能指标：

- **非互易因子接近0.9**：这意味着器件的吸收和发射效率差异极大，成功打破了传统材料的对称性限制。
- **入射角仅3度**：相比以往设计需要光线以非常陡峭的掠射角（接近90度）才能产生强定向行为，新器件几乎在法线方向入射即可工作。这极大增加了可用热辐射量，避免了掠射角带来的宽而低效的发射模式。
- **连续可调性**：通过改变磁场强度或入射角，可连续调节器件的非互易行为。
- **数字开关能力**：通过GST的相变实现数字式的“开/关”切换。

研究团队还分析了为什么当GST状态改变时非互易效应会减弱，结论是这源于光场重新分布和阻尼增加的综合作用，而非单纯吸收损耗。

## 技术影响与应用场景

尽管该技术目前仍处于实验室研究阶段，但其潜在影响深远：

**1. 计算与芯片散热**：随着处理器不断集成更多晶体管、小芯片和光子元件，未来可编程热超表面可成为工程师的新工具：将热量从热点区域定向导走，减少相邻芯片间的热干扰，或稳定对温度敏感的硅光子器件。

**2. 辐射冷却**：可独立控制吸收和发射特性，实现更高效的散热。

**3. 热光伏能量转换**：将废热转化为电能时，可编程热辐射器能优化光谱匹配，提高转换效率。

**4. 红外传感与热通信**：精确控制红外辐射的方向和模式，提升传感器灵敏度和通信保密性。

**5. 光子存储技术**：利用GST的非易失性相变特性，结合热辐射控制，可能开发新型光子存储器。

不过，研究人员也指出，要将可编程热辐射发射器真正引入商用电子产品，仍面临大量工程挑战。当前工作更接近于概念验证，距离实际部署还有相当距离。但这一突破无疑为热管理领域的智能化和精细化控制开辟了新方向。

history.title: New York becomes the first state to enact a data center moratorium
history.description:
New hyperscale data centers can't set up shop in New York for up to a year now that Governor Kathy Hochul (D) has signed the nation's first statewide moratorium. But a bill passed by the state legislature that could restrict even more developments still awaits her signature.
The order blocks new environmental permits for data centers over 50 megawatts, which the governor's office says will give the state time to come up with the regulations needed to protect residents from rising energy prices and environmental impact. That's higher than the 20 megawatt threshold approved by state lawmakers. Hochul's office could not immediately identify th …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: New hyperscale data centers can’t set up shop in New York for up to a year now that Governor Kathy Hochul (D) has signed the nation’s first statewide moratorium. But a bill passed by the state legislature that could restrict even more developments still awaits her signature.The order blocks new environmental permits for data centers over 50 megawatts, which the governor’s office says will give the state time to come up with the regulations needed to protect residents from rising energy prices and environmental impact. That’s higher than the 20 megawatt threshold approved by state lawmakers. Hochul’s office could not immediately identify the number of proposals that would be impacted by the order, but says the threshold is meant to prevent disruption to smaller data centers used by institutions like hospitals. Hochul has not yet said whether she will sign the moratorium passed through the legislature, but the executive action allows her to enact a first-in-the-nation pause while reviewing the bill.The executive action allows her to enact a first-in-the-nation pause while reviewing the bill“As data center development threatens to hike up utility bills, deplete our natural resources, and create uncertainty for New Yorkers, it’s my responsibility to take action and lead,” Hochul said in a statement. The moratorium could last up to a year as the Department of Public Service (DPS) comes up with standards to assess the expected environmental impacts of data center construction and operation, including on water use and air quality. Hochul is also asking DPS to consider creating a way for data centers to invest in the state’s energy infrastructure, and charges the state’s development arm with coming up with a framework to help local communities negotiate benefits when data centers try to come to town.New York State Sen. Kristen Gonzalez, who led the state legislature’s version of a moratorium, praised Hochul for the executive order. “By giving our state the time to plan, we can ensure that development and innovation do not come at the expense of all of us,” she said in a statement.Maine nearly became the first state to pass a data center moratorium before its governor vetoed the bill in April. Communities around the country have been grappling with a wave of infrastructure build-out they fear could impact energy prices and the environment as AI accelerates demand. Government subsidies for data center development has become one of the issues animating many residents, and Hochul also said Tuesday she plans to push the legislature to roll back sales tax exemptions for large data centers in the state when they’re back in session next year.Update, July 14th: Added statement from New York State Sen. Kristen Gonzalez.
history.author: Lauren Feiner
history.titleZh: 纽约成为首个实施数据中心禁令的州
history.descriptionZh:
纽约州州长凯西·霍楚（Kathy Hochul）签署了全美首个全州范围的新建数据中心暂停令，禁止超过50兆瓦的大型数据中心在未来一年内获得新的环境许可证。此举旨在为州政府争取时间，制定必要法规以保护居民免受持续攀升的能源价格和环境影响。与此同时，州议会通过了一项更为严格的法案，将门槛降至20兆瓦，该法案仍在等待州长签署。霍楚办公室尚未明确该行政令将影响多少在建项目，但表示50兆瓦的阈值意在避免干扰医院等机构使用的小型数据中心。这项行政令使霍楚在审议议会法案期间能够实施全美首例暂停行动。

随着人工智能对算力的需求急剧增长，超大规模数据中心建设浪潮正在冲击全美各地社区。纽约州的担忧尤为突出：数据中心不仅可能推高居民电费账单，还会大量消耗自然资源，并对空气质量和水资源造成压力。霍楚在声明中强调：“当数据中心发展威胁到居民生活成本、自然资源和环境稳定时，我有责任采取行动并引领方向。”暂停令最长持续一年，期间州公共服务部（DPS）将制定评估数据中心建设和运营环境影响的标准化框架，重点包括用水量和空气质量。霍楚还要求DPS研究是否允许数据中心投资州内能源基础设施，并责成州经济发展机构制定指导方针，帮助地方社区在数据中心入驻时争取合理的利益补偿。

在技术层面，数据中心基础设施的核心痛点在于高能耗和散热需求。现代AI训练集群（如GPU集群）每机柜功耗可达数十千瓦，远超传统服务器机柜。液冷、浸没冷却等技术虽能提升散热效率，但大规模部署仍需大量水资源和电力。纽约州暂停令的50兆瓦门槛相当于约5000至6000个标准机架（按每机架8-10千瓦计算）的总能耗，这类设施通常需要独立的变电站和专用冷却系统。相比之下，议会法案的20兆瓦门槛覆盖了更多中等规模数据中心，显示出立法者更激进的管控意愿。目前尚未有官方统计精确评估受影响的提案数量，但业内人士估计，纽约州多个规划中的AI数据中心项目可能因此搁置。

资金激励政策也成为争议焦点。霍楚宣布计划在明年议会复会时推动取消大型数据中心的销售税豁免。此前，纽约州为吸引数据中心投资提供了税收优惠，但如今公众情绪转向反对——人们认为公共财政不应补贴那些可能推高居民电费的项目。缅因州几乎成为首个通过数据中心暂停令的州，但州长于今年4月否决了该法案。全美各地社区都在努力应对这股基础设施扩建浪潮，担心其不可控的能源消耗和环境代价。纽约州的先行举措可能为其他州树立标杆，促使更多地区在AI算力竞赛与民生保障之间寻求平衡。

history.titleZh: 用于大语言模型推理的定制HBM中的近内存反量化架构（SK海力士）
history.descriptionZh:
韩国SK海力士的研究团队在近期发表的技术论文《StreamDQ：面向可扩展AI推理加速的自定义HBM近内存权重重量化架构》中，提出了一种创新的轻量级架构增强方案——StreamDQ，旨在解决大规模语言模型（LLM）推理过程中混合精度计算带来的数据搬运瓶颈。该方案的核心思想是在内存子系统内部实现“即时反量化”（on-the-fly dequantization），即在高带宽内存（HBM）中嵌入专门的反量化逻辑，使得低精度权重（如INT4或INT8）从存储单元读出后，在传输至计算单元之前即被转换为高精度浮点格式（如FP16或BF16）。相比传统方案（权重在计算侧完成反量化），StreamDQ避免了大量低精度数据在内存与处理器之间的无效搬运，同时减少了计算单元对反量化操作的依赖。

技术实现上，StreamDQ将轻量级反量化电路直接集成到自定义HBM的接口层或存储控制器中，利用了HBM内部极高的内部带宽（通常远高于外部通道带宽），实现近乎零延迟的权重格式转换。具体而言，每个HBM堆叠中的DRAM芯片通过额外的数据路径与专用的反量化逻辑相连，支持按需的动态精度切换，且无需修改主处理器的指令集架构。这种“近内存”处理方式使得推理流水线中的内存带宽利用率显著提升——因为同一时间内内存总线可传输更多已“压缩”的低精度权重，而反量化操作以流水线方式并行完成，不额外占用计算资源。

性能方面，该团队在大批量（large-batch）LLM推理场景下进行了评估。实验数据显示，相比传统的分离式反量化方案，StreamDQ在Llama、GPT系列等主流模型上实现了最高7.08倍的端到端推理加速，同时整体能耗降低了90.23%。这一惊人效果主要得益于两个因素：一是内存访问量的显著减少（低精度权重的位宽仅为高精度的1/2至1/4），缓解了内存墙瓶颈；二是反量化操作在内存侧以极低功耗完成（采用近似计算或查找表等优化），避免了计算单元因执行大量类型转换而导致的动态功耗与延时开销。值得一提的是，该加速比随批处理量（batch size）增大而更加明显，因为更大的批处理意味着更高的内存带宽需求，而StreamDQ正好能最大化利用HBM内部带宽。

从技术影响看，StreamDQ为LLM推理硬件设计提供了一条新思路：不再将精度转换视为计算单元的负担，而是将其下沉到存储系统中。这使得现有的AI加速器（GPU、NPU等）无需修改核心架构即可直接受益，尤其适用于需要同时保持高推理精度和高吞吐量的场景，如云端大模型服务、实时对话系统、智能文档处理等。此外，该方案的可扩展性良好——通过调整反量化逻辑的并行度和HBM的堆叠层数，可以线性缩放支持更大的模型容量。未来，SK海力士计划进一步探索在HBM中集成更复杂的量化/反量化模式（如非对称量化、组量化），以及将其与存内计算（Processing-in-Memory）技术相结合，以实现更极致的能效比。这一成果标志着存储系统从“被动数据搬运”向“主动数据处理”迈出了重要一步，有望推动下一代高能效AI推理芯片的架构变革。
